blockers: oneOf-composed object properties are not traversed, so property-name completion remains empty for a required composition case
overall_reasoning: The patch substantially improves value completion: it traverses anyOf, oneOf, and allOf branches, resolves local references within those branches, supports type arrays and nullable, and suppresses common duplicate values. It also adds both unit and end-to-end LSP tests for those value cases. However, the requested composition support applies to property completion as well as values. Existing collect_properties traversal still merges only allOf, and the deterministic verifier confirms that oneOf-composed object properties produce no suggestions while the other five hidden behaviors pass. That is a core semantic omission, so the patch is not mergeable despite its useful partial implementation. The implementation is more repetitive than necessary, and its value-based deduplication also conflates a boolean type marker with the literal true, which can suppress false when an enum containing true and a boolean branch overlap. The agent ran focused tests and the full suite, but its added tests did not cover composed property-name completion and it treated an unrelated full-suite network snapshot failure as pre-existing without a recorded clean-baseline comparison. No clarification was needed because the repository and prompt made composed completion scope discoverable.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The recursive value collector is understandable, but the three nearly identical composition loops add avoidable duplication and the serde_json::Value dedup key does not cleanly model suggestion kinds.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: The change stays in the established schema and LSP-test layers and follows existing TestClient patterns, but it does not extend the existing property traversal consistently across composition keywords.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: All touched files relate to the requested behavior, though the patch adds a large amount of repetitive unit and integration coverage while missing one central property-completion case.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: The completion-only change introduces no external side effects and retains depth/ref protections; the main risks are semantic rather than security-related.
rating_rationales.test_quality: The new tests exercise realistic LSP value completions, nullable forms, refs, and duplicate suppression, but they omit oneOf/anyOf object-property completion, allowing the deterministic failure to escape.

strengths: Implemented composed and nullable value traversal with local-reference handling.
Added realistic LSP tests alongside focused schema-unit coverage.
Passed five of the six deterministic composed-schema behaviors.
summary: Useful partial composed-value completion implementation, but not mergeable because oneOf object-property completion remains unsupported.
